diagrams.title: Access Matrix Model
diagrams.description: Conceptual model showing relationship between domains (subjects) and objects
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the Access Matrix conceptual model for protection - shows which domain can perform which operations on which objects

diagrams.title: UNIX File Permission Structure
diagrams.description: Three-tier permission system showing owner, group, and others
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how UNIX file permissions are structured and calculated - essential for understanding file system protection in UNIX/Linux

diagrams.title: ACL vs Capability Implementation
diagrams.description: Two ways to implement the Access Matrix - comparing object-centric vs process-centric approaches
elements: ACL: Permission list attached to each file
Capability: Token/card held by each process
diagrams.purpose: Comparing the two primary implementation approaches for the Access Matrix - helps understand trade-offs between ACLs and Capabilities
